data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Tesla, Qualcomm and Biogen among top picks
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the green on Friday after the week saw Iran declaring the Strait of Hormuz open for commercial vessels, along with earnings reports from streaming pioneer Netflix and healthcare giant Johnson & Johnson.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) both rose 3.9% and 0.8%, respectively.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Qualcomm downgraded by JPM, placed in ‘Negative Catalyst Watch’ list

Qualcomm (QCOM) was downgraded to Neutral from Overweight by J.P. Morgan, citing a combination of rising competition in the data center space and downside risks to estimates in the near term.

The financial firm also lowered its PT to $140 from $185 and placed the stock on its "Negative Catalyst Watch" list.

“While the company is still working on its roadmap with customers in building pipeline of opportunities across both datacenter CPUs and NPUs, recent announcements from Arm (ARM) and Nvidia (NVDA) focused on Datacenter CPUs as well as chipsets to address inference use cases are increasing the competitive intensity and driving us to look more for execution datapoints from Qualcomm, which we expect will take time to emerge,” said analyst Samik Chatterjee.

In the smartphone market, J.P. Morgan expects Qualcomm to face near-term pressure due to the memory supply shortage combined with its share loss from two of its premier customers, Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (SSNLF), while the brokerage also pointed out macroeconomic concerns related to Qualcomm's Internet of Things and Automotive business segments.

Tesla upgraded on favorable risk-reward profile

UBS upgraded Tesla (TSLA) to Neutral from Sell and raised its price target to $352, citing a more favorable risk-reward profile.

The bank noted that the stock’s current valuation now better reflects the tug-of-war between immediate electric vehicle demand hurdles and the massive long-term potential of Tesla’s “physical AI” initiatives.

"We think Tesla stock trades more on sentiment, narrative, and momentum than fundamentals. Recent concerns over EV demand, a 1Q26 energy shortfall, higher costs, higher capital spending requirements, and slow progress of robo-taxi and Optimus have weighed on the stock," analyst Joseph Spak said in a note, as per reports. "So we expect the stock may continue to exhibit high volatility."

Biogen raised to Overweight on Apellis buyout

Piper Sandler upgraded Biogen (BIIB) to Overweight from Neutral and increased its price target to $214 from $174, citing positives in the company’s recent agreement to acquire eye drug developer Apellis Pharmaceuticals (APLS).

Piper analyst David Amsellem argued that by adding Empaveli and Syfovre into its portfolio, the company will, at a minimum, fill a revenue gap created by the loss of exclusivity for its legacy multiple sclerosis assets.

“Given that backdrop, we can envision multiple expansion from a current EV/2026E EBITDA of ~11x,” the analyst wrote, assuming a 13x EV/2027E EBITDA multiple for the stock compared to 9x previously.

Susquehanna raises PT for Intel ahead of quarterly results

 Ahead of quarterly results on April 26, Susquehanna upped its price target for Intel (INTC) to $80 from $65, citing “insatiable” CPU demand.

“We expect in-line to slightly better 1Q26 results driven by stronger Server CPU demand, partially offset by weaker PC ODM builds,” analyst Christopher Rolland wrote.

“For Server, checks remain constructive as agentic AI workloads drive an inflection in CPU demand,” Rolland added.  “Importantly, Intel noted it is unable to meet the strong demand as supply constraints are expected to peak in 1Q. However, it expects these constraints to clear starting in 2Q, leading to above-seasonal results quarterly through the rest of 2026 (we believe primarily driven by Server).”

Despite the server strength, Rolland, who still has a Neutral rating on Intel, noted that memory shortages are hurting PC builds, which could negatively impact Intel.

Macquarie analysts Ellie Jiang and Jason Zhang upgraded JD.com (JD) to Outperform from Neutral and set a $35 price target, ahead of Q1 results.

“We expect [JD Retail] to be largely on track, with the high base effect from the trade-in policies dragging on electronics’ sales, offset by faster-growing advertising revenue as [JD Retail] expands into more third-party categories,” Jiang and Zhang said.

Networking names Corning (GLW) and Fabrinet (FN) were both downgraded to Neutral from Overweight by J.P. Morgan. "In our view, this valuation creates a challenging execution hurdle relative to the earnings expectations now being embedded by the buyside to justify such a lofty premium," said JPM analysts. However, JPM increased its PT, bumping up Corning to $175 from $115 and Fabrinet to $700 from $530.

data.title: Morgan Stanley, Citi in gainers; Wells Fargo among losers: week's financials wrap
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages posted big weekly gains, surging to new highs for three consecutive trading days, as positive developments in the Middle East—including a temporary ceasefire deal between the involved countries—raised investors' enthusiasm.

The week saw investors shrug off fears of a potential bubble in the artificial intelligence industry. Around $242B, or 80% of the total global venture funding in Q1, went to AI companies, Crunchbase data showed.

The financial sector has had a bad start to 2026, negatively impacted by a crisis in the private credit sector and AI risk concerns, which hit the wealth and asset management space, insurance brokerage industry, and small banks, among others.

The stocks of card payment networks were also lagging on the back of concerns that stablecoin-based technology will obviate the need for traditional card networks.

However, a strong start to the first-quarter earnings season helped the financials sector post strong weekly gains. Major U.S. banks are on track to post more than $40B in trading revenue for the first quarter, as heightened geopolitical tensions helped drive sharp movements across financial markets.

State Street Financial Sel Sec SPDR ETF (XLF) added 3.27% during the course of the week.

Among the megacap stocks, Morgan Stanley (MS) and Citigroup (C), having reported a Q1 beat, led the weekly gainers. MS added 6.29% to $188.82, while C advanced 6.26% to $132.18.

Trading desks of Wall Street banks notched new milestones with their first-quarter results, capitalizing on market volatility fueled by the Iran war.

JPMorgan (JPM), Citi (C) and Wells Fargo (WFC) together reported over $25B of profits for the first quarter, as their traders benefited from sharp market moves driven by geopolitical shocks in Venezuela and Iran.

American Express (AXP) was next on the list, advancing 5.80% from the prior week to close at $331.69.

This week, American Express announced an enhanced focus on its AI capabilities, extending its card member purchase protections to agentic purchases as it also introduced its agentic commerce developer kit as well as agreeing to acquire Hypercard, known as Hyper, to add to its AI expertise and expense management capabilities.

Ironically, Wells Fargo (WFC) was the top decliner in the category, retreating 4.67% to $81.41. The banking giant reported net interest income that fell short of analyst expectations.

Among largecap stocks, Affirm Holdings (AFRM) (+32.91% W/W to $64.50) led the gainers.

The stock surged after Morgan Stanley named the company a top pick in the consumer finance sector.

Moreover, news that Iran has opened the Strait of Hormuz likely buoyed fintech and consumer finance stocks, as lower gasoline prices may free up cash for consumers to spend in other areas or give them more confidence to borrow.

Other fintech and consumer finance stocks were also rising, including Robinhood Markets (HOOD). HOOD advanced 31.16% during the week to $90.75.

Furthermore, crypto stocks rallied on the news about the opening of the Strait of Hormuz. Coinbase Global (COIN) (+22.93% W/W to $206.33) and IREN (IREN) (+22.38% W/W to $48.12) were the notable crypto gainers.

Meanwhile, Credicorp (BAP) (-5.00% W/W to $331.94) led the losers in the category.

For the midcap stocks, Chinese fintech Maase (MAAS) saw the biggest advance of 83.93% to $9.84. Investors piled into the stock after a rebound in sentiment surrounding the AI industry.

Sezzle (SEZL) followed, with a 35.18% advance to $80.71.

Strive Asset Management (ASST) (+46.85% W/W to $15.61) and American Bitcoin (ABTC) (+36.53% W/W to $1.30) topped the smallcap gainers.

Strive was initiated with a Buy rating and $26 price target at TD Cowen a week before.

Isabella Bank (ISBA) led the losers, pulling back 6.06% to $45.75.

World Acceptance (WRLD) (-4.99% W/W to $141.38) was a notable loser. The stock lost after its board appointed Janet Matricciani as interim president and CEO as R. Chad Prashad resigned from the post. Prashad resigned to pursue other opportunities.

data.title: Trump issues executive order on psychedelic drugs
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an executive order to expedite access to research and treatments ​based on psychedelic drugs in a bid to ease restrictions on substances such as psilocybin, ibogaine, LSD, and MDMA.

The order will “dramatically accelerate access to new medical research and treatments based on psychedelic drugs,” Trump said in the Oval Office event attended by top government officials, including Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.

“In many cases, these experimental treatments have shown life-changing potential for those suffering from severe mental illness and depression—including our cherished veterans,” Trump added.

Shares of companies focused on developing psychedelic drugs rose on Thursday after CBS News reported that Trump plans to sign an executive order that would expand research into ibogaine, a psychedelic plant medicine native to Africa.

Notable companies focused on psychedelic drugs: Atai Life Sciences (ATAI), COMPASS Pathways (CMPS), GH Research (GHRS), Definium Therapeutics (DFTX), and Cybin (HELP).

data.title: Nexstar-Tegna TV merger in doubt as judge blocks the $6.2B deal
data.description:
A federal judge in California has blocked Nexstar Media Group’s (NXST) $6.2B deal to acquire rival broadcast station group TEGNA (TGNA), casting doubts on a transaction that would create the largest local TV operator in the U.S.

U.S. District Court Chief Judge Troy L. Nunley in Sacramento issued a preliminary injunction late Friday extending a temporary order he imposed last month that paused the integration between two of the largest TV station groups in the U.S.

“Nexstar must permit Tegna to continue operating as a separate and distinct, independently managed business unit from Nexstar,” Nunley argued, issuing the order, which takes effect on Tuesday. “And Nexstar must put measures in place to maintain Tegna as an ongoing, economically viable, and active competitor,” he added in his 52-page ruling.

Nunley argued that allowing the merger would harm plaintiffs, TPG (TPG)-owned DirecTV, and eight state attorneys general while reducing competition and potentially leading to newsroom layoffs.

The lawsuits pointed out that after the deal, Nexstar, already the largest owner of local broadcast stations in the nation, would expand to more than 80% of U.S. households. The company said it would appeal the ruling.

Tegna (TGNA) and Nexstar (NXST) closed the merger in March after receiving regulatory clearances from the Federal Communications Commission and the Department of Justice.

data.title: Bally's is in advanced talks to acquire Evoke - The Times
data.description:
Bally's Corporation (BALY) is in advanced talks to acquire Evoke plc (EIHDF) in a take-private “rescue” deal that could be announced as soon as this week if negotiations continue to progress, according to The Times.

Evoke plc is an international betting and gaming group that owns brands such as William Hill, 888casino, 888sport, 888poker, Mr Green, and Winner.ro. Headquartered in Gibraltar and listed in London, it operates retail and online businesses across the core markets of UK, Italy, Spain, Romania, and Denmark. The company was founded in 1997 and rebranded from 888 Holdings to Evoke in 2024 to reflect its multi-brand strategy.

In terms of a potential deal, Bally's (BALY) has been given informal preferred-bidder status by Evoke after a sales process run by advisers Morgan Stanley and Rothschild, which the company launched in December as it explored strategic alternatives to restore shareholder value. Notably, the company is struggling under about £1.7B of debt against a market valuation of roughly £160 million. The financial pressure is largely from its £2.2B acquisition in 2021 of William Hill’s non-US assets from Caesars Entertainment (CZR).

data.title: Earnings Scoreboard: 85% of S&P 500 early reporters beat EPS estimates as Y/Y growth hits 25 firms
data.description:
S&P 500 earnings moved into high gear this week, with 28 companies across the financial, healthcare, industrial, and staples sectors providing a vital barometer for the U.S. economy. The results signaled broad-based strength, particularly in investment banking and AI-driven technology infrastructure, where early reporters set a high bar for the rest of the season.

Earnings Roundup:

Bottom Line: Out of the 28 companies reporting, 24 exceeded EPS expectations, three met estimates, and only one fell short. Notably, 25 firms posted year-over-year earnings growth.

Top Line: Performance remained robust on the revenue front, with 22 companies topping estimates while six missed. On a year-over-year basis, 26 of the 28 companies reported revenue expansion.

 

 

Let’s take a look at some of the companies that reported earnings this week:

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) stock fell 0.9% in premarket trading after lowering its 2026 net interest income (NII) guidance. Q1 earnings and revenue exceeded analyst expectations due to growth in various bank sectors and lower credit loss provisions. The bank now projects NII of ~$103B, while reaffirming adjusted expenses at ~$105B and a card service net charge-off rate of ~3.4%.

Goldman Sachs (GS) stock fell 3.5% in premarket trading due to lower Q4 net interest income and higher credit loss provisions. However, Q1 GAAP EPS was $17.55, exceeding analyst estimates and rising from previous quarters, with total revenue at $17.2B, beating expectations.Wells Fargo (WFC) had mixed results, with a revenue miss impacting its stock, which fell by 4.92%. The bank reported an EPS of $1.60, surpassing the $1.58 estimate, but total revenue of $21.4 billion was below the forecast of $21.76 billion.Citigroup (C) stock rose 1.5% in premarket trading after the bank confirmed its 2026 net interest income guidance and reported strong Q1 earnings. The bank expects a 5%-6% rise in full-year 2026 NII, excluding markets, and aims for a 10-11% RoTCE. Q1 GAAP EPS was $3.06, surpassing estimates. Q1 revenue reached $24.6B, exceeding the consensus.Bank of America (BAC) Q1 earnings exceeded Wall Street predictions due to a revival in investment banking and increased sales and trading revenue from market changes. Net interest income rose 9%, contributing to a 7% year-over-year revenue growth. Q1 EPS was $1.11, surpassing analyst expectations of $1.02, with revenue at $30.3 billion. 

Morgan Stanley (MS) stock rose 2.6% in premarket trading after better-than-expected Q1 earnings. Q1 GAAP EPS was $3.43, above the $3.02 estimate, up from $2.68 in Q4 2025. Net revenue reached $20.6B, surpassing $19.8B estimates. Net interest income was $2.70B, below the $2.77B consensus.

BlackRock (BLK) exceeded Q1 expectations, with assets under management rising to $13.9 trillion and quarterly net inflows of $130 billion. The firm reported an adjusted EPS of $12.53, surpassing the $11.48 consensus. Revenue grew 27% to $6.7 billion.

Shares of Netflix (NFLX) fell late Thursday after strong first-quarter results were overshadowed by cautious guidance. The company reported $1.23 EPS, exceeding the $0.76 consensus, but issued a weaker revenue outlook for the second quarter. Co-founder Reed Hastings will not seek re-election as board chairman this June.

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) raised its full-year guidance after Q1 financials beat expectations, driven by cancer drug sales, although Crohn's disease therapy sales fell. The company reported $24.1B in revenue, surpassing estimates by $450M, and adjusted earnings per share were $2.70, exceeding estimates by $0.02. An Enterprise Business Review is scheduled for Dec. 8, as the company aims for double-digit growth by the end of the decade.

Shares of Abbott Laboratories (ABT) tumbled on Thursday despite the company meeting earnings estimates and narrowly beating on revenue. The MedTech giant lowered its full-year earnings outlook, indicating an impact from its recent acquisition of cancer diagnostics company Exact Sciences.

PepsiCo (PEP) reported improved results in its food segment for the first quarter after committing to lower some prices. Revenue increased by 8.5% to $19.4B, with organic sales up 2.6%, surpassing expectations. The Europe, Middle East, and Africa segment grew by 7%, while North America only increased by 1%. Food volume rose by 4%, but beverage volume remained flat.

Earnings next week

The Q1 earnings season shifts into high gear next week as 86 S&P 500 companies are set to report. The Industrials and Financials sectors will lead these reports. Investors are particularly focused on major companies like Tesla (TSLA), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), and General Electric (GE) on Tuesday and Wednesday, impacting the tech, automotive, and healthcare sectors. Key updates will follow from Boeing (BA), AT&T (T), and International Business Machines (IBM), ending with insights from Procter & Gamble (PG) and American Express (AXP).

Other major companies scheduled to disclose their first-quarter results include Intel (INTC), Blackstone (BX), 3M (MMM), Honeywell (HON), and ServiceNow (NOW), alongside aerospace leaders like Lockheed Martin (LMT) and RTX Corporation (RTX).

More on markets

data.title: U.S. renews waiver on Russian oil sanctions
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury Department has extended a waiver that will temporarily ease some sanctions on Russian oil shipments just two days after Secretary Scott Bessent said Washington would not renew the exemption despite surging oil prices caused by Middle Eastern tensions.

The so-called general license means U.S. sanctions will not apply for 30 days on deliveries of Russian oil that has been loaded on tankers as of Friday. It extended a similar 30-day license issued in March for Russian oil that had been loaded by March 11.

The extension underscores how the fallout from the Iran war has boosted Moscow’s ability to profit from its energy exports, which had been restrained since the invasion of Ukraine.

Speaking at the White House on Wednesday, Bessent ruled out extending the license. “We will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil,” he said. The administration did not immediately explain the reversal.

The AP contributed to this report

Relevant tickers: (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM), (NG1:COM), (XB1:COM), (XLE), (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: Tanker comes under fire as Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again
data.description:
The British military announced on Saturday that a tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz had come under fire from two gunboats operated by Iran’s Revolutionary Guard. The incident occurred after Tehran announced earlier in the day that it would reimpose restrictions on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, as the U.S. blockade of the vital maritime trade route continued.

Confusion over the critical chokepoint threatened to deepen the energy crisis roiling the global economy and push the two countries toward renewed conflict, even as mediators expressed confidence a new deal was within reach.

Iran’s joint military command said on Saturday that “control of the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state ... under strict management and control of the armed forces.” It warned that it would continue to block transit through the strait as long as the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports remained in effect.

Two gunboats from Iran’s Revolutionary Guard opened fire on a tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz, the British military's United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said on Saturday. It reported the tanker and crew as safe, without identifying the vessel or its destination. TankerTrackers.com reported vessels were forced to turn around in the strait, including an Indian-flagged super tanker, after they were fired on by Iran.

Iran announced earlier Saturday it was reimposing restrictions on the strait in response to a U.S. blockade on Iranian shipping and ports. Iran has prevented vessels from crossing throughout the seven-week-long war, except for ones it authorizes.

Ebrahim Azizi, head of the Iranian parliament’s National Security Commission, said that the strait was “returning to the status quo,” which he had earlier described as ships requiring Iranian naval authorization and toll payment before transiting.

The shift came a day after Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared the strait open while a 10-day truce was announced between Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon. An end to Israel’s war with Hezbollah was a key demand of Iranian negotiators, who previously accused Israel of breaking last week’s ceasefire with strikes on Lebanon. Israel had said that deal did not cover Lebanon.

U.S. President Donald Trump first appeared to take a similar position on reopening the strait before later saying the American blockade “will remain in full force” regardless of what Iran does until a deal is reached, including about Iran's nuclear program.

Even as the U.S.-Iran ceasefire appeared to hold, the back-and-forth over the strait—through which roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil typically passes—highlighted how easily it could unravel

Control over the strait has proven to be one Iran’s main points of leverage and prompted the United States to deploy forces and initiate a blockade on Iranian ports as part of an effort to force Iran to accept a Pakistan-brokered ceasefire to end almost seven weeks of war that has raged between Israel, the U.S. and Iran.

A data firm, Kpler, said movement through the strait remained confined to corridors requiring Iran’s approval. U.S. forces have sent 21 ships back to Iran since the blockade began on Monday, U.S. Central Command said on X.

Relevant tickers: (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM), (NG1:COM), (XB1:COM), (XLE), (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

The AP contributed to this report.

Dear readers, we recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Run, Robot, Run: Investors eye Beijing's humanoid half-marathon for breakouts
data.description:
Humanoid robots will run alongside thousands of humans in a half-marathon through an industrial-technology park in Beijing on April 19. More than 300 robots are expected to start the race this year, compared to just 21 that ran the race last year.

Notably, Alibaba Group's (BABA) Amap mapping unit plans to launch its first embodied robot at the Beijing Humanoid Robot Half Marathon. The company has utilized a strategy of building the software foundation first, then using that foundation to power different robot bodies.

Honor said it will send its self-developed robots “Lightning” and “Energetic Boy,” while Unitree is also participating this year with its H1 model in mostly autonomous mode. HONOR was acquired by Shenzhen Zhixin New Information Technology Co. in 2020 from Huawei. 

Unitree is also participating this year in the humanoid half-marathon race with its H1 model in mostly autonomous mode. Unitree Robotics was founded in 2016 by Wang Xingxing, a robotics engineer whose early work focused on developing affordable and agile legged robots, starting with the XDog prototype during his graduate studies at Shanghai University. The company quickly gained attention for its quadruped robots and its stated goal to offer advanced robotics at a fraction of the cost of Western competitors.

Last year’s winner was Tiangong Ultra from the Beijing Humanoid Robot Innovation Center, a project linked to Ubtech Robotics (UBTRF), Xiaomi (XIACF) (XIACY) and other partners. 

The half-marathon is much more than just a test of how fast and how long a humanoid robot can run. The entries will be watched for heat management, navigation, battery swaps, and recovery from falls. This year’s shift toward autonomous navigation is especially important because it moves the competition from controlled demos toward something closer to commercial deployment. The proof-of-concept could also be critical for some of the company looking for capital.

The race may also display the lead Chinese firms have in linking embodied AI and humanoid robots, as well as be a test case for future applications. The event could also throw a spotlight on the progress with Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot.

Related humanoid robotics ETFs: Roundhill Humanoid Robotics ETF (HUMN), KraneShares Global Humanoid & Embodied Intelligence Index ETF (KOID), Themes Humanoid Robotics ETF (BOTT), Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF (NASDAQ:BOTZ), and ROBO Global Robotics & Automation Index ETF (ROBO).

data.title: What is next for Bank Of America after Q1 earnings?
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) reported stronger-than-expected Q1 2026 earnings on April 15, with its shares closing 1.8% higher. The banking giant reported net interest income of $15.75 billion and demonstrated robust loan growth of 1.7% for the quarter and 8.6% year-over-year, while maintaining a conservative 58% loan-to-deposit ratio.

Despite the strong results, analyst sentiment reflects cautious optimism, with expectations heavily tied to the Federal Reserve’s interest rate trajectory in the months ahead.

What Do Seeking Alpha Analysts Say About Bank Of America’s Future?

Supporters pointed to the “higher-for-longer” rate environment as a key catalyst for Bank of America’s earnings outlook, noting that net interest income is positioned to benefit through at least FY27. Analysts highlighted the company’s universal banking model, scale, and strong funding base as structural advantages supporting resilient earnings. The bank’s disciplined rate management resulted in a 5-basis-point improvement in net interest spread to 1.38%.

Critics, however, highlighted potential risks, including accelerated Fed rate cuts should inflation or energy prices decline. Credit quality deterioration remains a concern, as it would necessitate higher provisions and reduce profitability. Some analysts also noted the sensitivity of net interest income to unexpected shifts in short-term rates.

Here’s a breakdown of what some analysts had to say:

  • Luca Socci, Rating: Buy: “BAC stands out for its universal banking model, scale, and strong funding base, supporting resilient earnings… NII is positioned to benefit from a higher-for-longer Fed rate environment, with NII expansion likely through at least FY27.” - Bank Of America: Fairly Valued Before Q1 Print, But Now Attractive
  • The Asian Investor, Rating: Buy: “A higher-for-longer interest rate environment, fueled by rising inflation and energy prices, is a key catalyst for BAC’s earnings outlook.” - Bank of America: A Higher-For-Longer Rate Play
  • Jeremy LaKosh, Rating: Buy: “BAC has maintained consistent loan growth—1.7% in Q1 and 8.6% year-over-year—outpacing deposit growth but keeping a conservative 58% loan-to-deposit ratio.” - Bank of America: Q1 Earnings Keep The Bullish Thesis Intact

What Do the Quant Ratings Say?

Bank of America maintains a strong quantitative standing following its Q1 2026 report. The company demonstrates resilient profitability and disciplined capital ratios despite broader rate volatility.

Profitability (A+): The bank continues to demonstrate resilient earnings, supported by its universal banking model and stable net interest income of $15.75 billion. The strong funding base and cross-selling capabilities across segments provide additional support for profitability metrics.

Valuation ((D)): BAC’s shares trade at a 1.37X price-to-book ratio, comparing favorably to peers like Wells Fargo (WFC). This valuation presents upside potential if the bank succeeds in growing its net interest income.

Growth (B+): The bank demonstrates robust growth metrics with loan growth of 8.6% year-over-year, outpacing deposit growth while maintaining a conservative 58% loan-to-deposit ratio.

Momentum (B-): Shares rose following the better-than-expected Q1 earnings report, reflecting positive market sentiment around the results.

Earnings Revisions ((B)): The Q1 print exceeded expectations, keeping the bullish thesis intact among analysts and supporting a constructive outlook for future quarters.

What’s the Latest News on Bank of America?

  • During the Q1 conference call, Executive VP & CFO Borthwick projected net interest income (NII) growth of 6% to 8%, reflecting the updated interest rate outlook, which now assumes no rate cuts compared to the previously expected two.
  • The CFO further noted that the company expects more than 200 basis points of positive operating leverage for the year, in line with prior guidance, along with an effective tax rate slightly above 20%.
  • The company plans to redeem on May 4, 2026, all €1.5B of the principal amount outstanding of its 1.776% Fixed/Floating Rate Senior Notes, due May 4, 2027.
